title: Events / sec (1m)
description: Total OCSF events per second over the last 1 minute.
viz_content: SELECT count() / 60.0 AS events_per_sec FROM table(ocsf.ocsf_events) WHERE _tp_time > now() - interval 1 minute

title: Auth failures (5m)
description: Authentication failure count over the last 5 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT count() AS failures FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten) WHERE status = 'Failure' AND _tp_time > now() - interval 5 minute

title: High+Critical findings (5m)
description: High and Critical severity security findings in the last 5 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT count() AS findings FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_security_finding_flatten) WHERE severity IN ('High','Critical') AND _tp_time > now() - interval 5 minute

title: Active source IPs (5m)
description: Distinct source IPs seen in authentication events over the last 5 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT count_distinct(src_endpoint_ip) AS unique_sources FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten) WHERE _tp_time > now() - interval 5 minute

title: Auth events by status / min
description: Authentication events grouped by status in 1-minute tumbling windows.
viz_content: SELECT window_start AS time, status, count() AS auths FROM tumble(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten, 1m) GROUP BY window_start, status

title: Top failed sources (15m)
description: Top source IPs with the most authentication failures in the last 15 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT src_endpoint_ip, src_location_country, count() AS failed FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten) WHERE status = 'Failure' AND _tp_time > now() - interval 15 minute GROUP BY src_endpoint_ip, src_location_country ORDER BY failed DESC LIMIT 10

title: Brute-force candidates (5m)
description: User/IP pairs with 3+ failures in 5-minute windows — potential brute-force attempts.
viz_content: SELECT window_start, user_name, src_endpoint_ip, count() AS failures FROM tumble(ocsf.v_ocsf_authentication_flatten, 5m) WHERE status = 'Failure' GROUP BY window_start, user_name, src_endpoint_ip HAVING failures >= 3 ORDER BY window_start DESC LIMIT 20

title: Bytes by protocol / min
description: Network traffic bytes per protocol in 1-minute tumbling windows.
viz_content: SELECT window_start AS time, protocol_name, sum(traffic_bytes) AS bytes FROM tumble(ocsf.v_ocsf_network_activity_flatten, 1m) GROUP BY window_start, protocol_name

title: High-severity events (15m)
description: High and Critical severity network events in the last 15 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT _tp_time, src_ip, dst_ip, protocol_name, severity, traffic_bytes FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_network_activity_flatten) WHERE severity IN ('High','Critical') AND _tp_time > now() - interval 15 minute ORDER BY _tp_time DESC LIMIT 20

title: Scanning candidates (2m)
description: Source IPs with 20+ attempts to 10+ distinct targets in 2-minute windows.
viz_content: SELECT window_start, src_ip, count() AS attempts, count_distinct(dst_ip) AS targets FROM tumble(ocsf.v_ocsf_network_activity_flatten, 2m) GROUP BY window_start, src_ip HAVING attempts >= 20 AND targets >= 10 ORDER BY window_start DESC LIMIT 20

title: Process creates / min
description: Count of process Create events in 1-minute tumbling windows.
viz_content: SELECT window_start AS time, count() AS creates FROM tumble(ocsf.v_ocsf_process_activity_flatten, 1m) WHERE activity_name = 'Create' GROUP BY window_start

title: Suspicious command lines (15m)
description: Process events matching suspicious command-line patterns in the last 15 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT _tp_time, device_hostname, process_name, process_cmd_line, process_user_name FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_process_activity_flatten) WHERE (process_cmd_line LIKE '%powershell%ExecutionPolicy Bypass%' OR process_cmd_line LIKE '%cmd.exe /c%' OR process_cmd_line LIKE '%rundll32%' OR process_name IN ('psexec.exe', 'mimikatz.exe', 'procdump.exe')) AND _tp_time > now() - interval 15 minute ORDER BY _tp_time DESC LIMIT 20

title: Privilege-mismatch events (15m)
description: Process events where process user type and actor user type differ, involving Admin.
viz_content: SELECT _tp_time, device_hostname, process_name, process_user_type, actor_user_type FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_process_activity_flatten) WHERE process_user_type != actor_user_type AND (process_user_type = 'Admin' OR actor_user_type = 'Admin') AND _tp_time > now() - interval 15 minute ORDER BY _tp_time DESC LIMIT 20

title: Findings by severity / min
description: Security findings by severity level in 1-minute tumbling windows.
viz_content: SELECT window_start AS time, severity, count() AS findings FROM tumble(ocsf.v_ocsf_security_finding_flatten, 1m) WHERE severity IN ('Critical','High','Medium') GROUP BY window_start, severity

title: Malware by classification (15m)
description: Malware detection counts grouped by classification in the last 15 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT malware_classification, count() AS detections, count_distinct(malware_name) AS unique_malware FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_security_finding_flatten) WHERE malware_name != '' AND _tp_time > now() - interval 15 minute GROUP BY malware_classification ORDER BY detections DESC

title: Top finding titles (15m)
description: Most frequently occurring security finding titles in the last 15 minutes.
viz_content: SELECT finding_title, count() AS occurrences, max(severity) AS max_severity FROM table(ocsf.v_ocsf_security_finding_flatten) WHERE _tp_time > now() - interval 15 minute GROUP BY finding_title ORDER BY occurrences DESC LIMIT 10

config.content:
### OCSF Security Event Analytics

Real-time analytics over the [Open Cybersecurity Schema Framework](https://schema.ocsf.io/) event stream emitted by the [`timeplus-ocsf-simulator`](https://pypi.org/project/timeplus-ocsf-simulator/) package.

Four event classes are flattened and surfaced above: **Authentication (3002)**, **Network Activity (4001)**, **Process Activity (1007)**, and **Security Finding (2001)**.

> Heads-up: the simulator generates random IPs and user names for each event, so cross-class correlations (e.g. linking an authentication failure to a subsequent suspicious process by IP) will not match. Each section above is self-contained per class.
